contents.value:
```ini
[section_name]
key1 = value1
key2 = value2
```
A **section** groups related configuration entries under a named header enclosed in square brackets.

```ini
port = 3306
bind-address = 127.0.0.1
```
**Key-value pair** - Assigns a configuration value to a named key. Delimiter is `=` or `:`.

```ini
; This is a comment
# This is also a comment
```
**Comments** start with `;` or `#`. The entire line after the comment character is ignored.

```ini
[DEFAULT]
base_dir = /opt

[app]
path = %(base_dir)s/myapp
```
The **[DEFAULT]** section provides fallback values that are inherited by all other sections.

```ini
[paths]
base = /srv
data = %(base)s/data
```
**Interpolation** substitutes `%(key)s` with the value of another key. Supported by Python ConfigParser.

```ini
description = This is a
  multiline value that
  spans several lines
```
**Multi-line values** - Continuation lines must be indented with whitespace.

```ini
[mysqld]
port = 3306
max_connections = 151
innodb_buffer_pool_size = 128M
```
**[mysqld]** - MySQL server daemon configuration section in my.cnf.

```ini
[PHP]
memory_limit = 128M
upload_max_filesize = 2M
max_execution_time = 30
```
**PHP configuration** in php.ini. Controls runtime behavior, memory limits, and file upload sizes.

```ini
[program:myapp]
command = /usr/bin/python app.py
autostart = true
autorestart = true
```
**Supervisor** process control system config. Manages long-running processes with auto-restart.
A **key** (or property name) identifies a configuration setting.
A **value** is the data assigned to a configuration key.
A **string value** contains text, optionally quoted with single or double quotes.
A **boolean value** represents true/false, on/off, yes/no.
An **integer value** is a whole number used for ports, counts, sizes.
An **array** stores multiple values for a single key.
An **include** directive loads configuration from another file.
A **variable** references another value using interpolation syntax.
A **block** groups nested configuration entries.
A **directive** is a configuration command that controls behavior.
**Environment variables** are external values available to configuration.
A **default value** is used when no explicit value is provided.
An **override** replaces a previously defined setting with a new value.

```ini
; comment text
```
A comment preceded by a semicolon. The entire line is ignored by the parser.

```ini
# comment text
```
A comment preceded by a hash sign. Supported by most INI parsers.

```ini
MySQL configuration file
```
MySQL/MariaDB server configuration file using INI format. Common sections: [mysqld], [client], [mysql].

```ini
PHP configuration file
```
PHP runtime configuration file. Controls memory limits, upload sizes, error reporting, extensions.

```ini
Supervisor config
```
Process manager configuration file. Defines programs to monitor, restart policies, and logging.

```ini
uWSGI config
```
uWSGI application server configuration. Defines WSGI module, workers, sockets, and process management.

```ini
~/.gitconfig
```
Git user configuration file using INI format. Sections include [user], [core], [alias], [remote].

```ini
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```ini
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```ini
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```ini
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```ini
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```ini
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```ini
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```ini
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```ini
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
